title: Rates and Unit Rates
contentObjective: I can find a unit rate to compare prices and decide the better buy.
languageObjective: I can explain my choice using the words rate, unit rate, per, and better buy.

turnAndTalk.question: Booth A charges $3 for 5 games and Booth B charges $5 for 8 games. Just by looking, which booth seems like the better deal, and what makes it hard to be sure?
turnAndTalk.kernel: It is hard to compare because the booths have different numbers of games.

stems.es: El puesto ___ se ve mejor porque ___.

stems.en: It is hard to compare because the ___ are different.
stems.es: Es difícil comparar porque los/las ___ son diferentes.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students notice the prices cannot be compared directly because the number of games differs, so they need the cost for one game (the unit rate) to compare fairly.
turnAndTalk.extend: Why can you not just pick the booth with the lower total price of $3? Explain what a fair comparison actually needs.
extendStems: You cannot just use the lower price because ___.
A fair comparison needs ___ so that ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How did you find the cost per game (the unit rate) for each booth, and what did you divide?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I divided the total cost by the number of games to get the cost for one game.

stems.en: The unit rate is ___ per one ___.
stems.es: La tasa unitaria es ___ por un ___.

stems.en: I divided ___ by ___ to get the cost per game.
stems.es: Dividí ___ entre ___ para obtener el costo por juego.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain they divided total cost by number of games (e.g., $5.00 div 8 = $0.625) and state the result as a cost per one game.
turnAndTalk.extend: Two booths in your table had different totals but the same unit rate. How is that possible, and what does it tell you about the deals?
extendStems: They can have the same unit rate because ___.
Even though the totals differ, the deals are ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: The snack bar sells popcorn as 3 cups for $2.25 or 8 cups for $5.60. How do unit rates help you decide the better buy?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I find the price per cup for each bag and pick the lower one.

stems.en: The ___ bag is the better buy because its unit rate is $___ per cup.
stems.es: La bolsa ___ es la mejor compra porque su tasa unitaria es $___ por taza.

stems.en: I divided ___ by ___ to find the price per cup.
stems.es: Dividí ___ entre ___ para hallar el precio por taza.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute $0.75/cup and $0.70/cup, and choose the large bag because the lower unit rate means each cup costs less.
turnAndTalk.extend: A friend says the large bag must be the better buy because it costs less per cup AND gives more popcorn. Is 'more popcorn' a fair reason to call it the better buy? Justify using the unit rate.
extendStems: 'More popcorn' is or is not a fair reason because ___.
The real reason it is the better buy is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Rates and Unit Rates, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

stems.en: The unit rate is ___ per one ___.
stems.es: La tasa unitaria es ___ por un ___.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.RP.2 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A ratio comparing two amounts with different units, like miles per hour.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una razón que compara dos cantidades con unidades distintas, como millas por hora.
vocabulary.cloze: A ratio that compares two quantities with different units is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Tỷ số so sánh hai đại lượng có đơn vị khác nhau.
vocabulary.definitionAr: نسبة تقارن بين كميتين لهما وحدتان مختلفتان.

vocabulary.definition: A rate for just 1 of something, like cost for 1 item.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una tasa para solo 1 de algo, como el precio de 1 artículo.
vocabulary.cloze: A rate that tells the cost or amount for one unit is a ___.

examples.why: It gives the amount for exactly one unit.
That is a rate for 3 units, not a unit rate.

vocabulary.termVi: Đơn giá / Tỷ số đơn vị
vocabulary.definitionVi: Tỷ số so sánh một lượng ứng với đúng một đơn vị của đại lượng khác.
vocabulary.definitionAr: معدل يقارن كمية بوحدة واحدة من كمية أخرى.

vocabulary.definition: For each one. Example: 5 dollars per book.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Por cada uno. Ejemplo: 5 dólares por libro.
vocabulary.visual: 60 miles per hour means 60 miles for every 1 hour
vocabulary.cloze: The word that means 'for each one' is ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Từ chỉ tỷ lệ trên mỗi đơn vị (ví dụ: số dặm trên mỗi giờ).
vocabulary.definitionAr: كلمة تعني لكل وحدة واحدة (مثال: ميل لكل ساعة).

vocabulary.definition: A way to compare two amounts.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de comparar dos cantidades.
vocabulary.cloze: A comparison of two quantities is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Sự so sánh hai đại lượng bằng phép chia (ví dụ: a/b hoặc a:b).
vocabulary.definitionAr: مقارنة بين كميتين باستخدام القسمة (مثال: a/b أو a:b).

vocabulary.definition: The choice that costs less for each item.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La opción que cuesta menos por cada artículo.
vocabulary.visual: Pack A: $0.50 per pencil vs Pack B: $0.40 per pencil — Pack B is the better buy
vocabulary.cloze: The option with the lower unit rate, costing less per item, is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Mặt hàng có đơn giá thấp hơn khi so sánh giá cả.
vocabulary.definitionAr: السلعة التي لها سعر وحدة أقل عند مقارنة الأسعار.

vocabulary.definition: Splitting a total into equal groups.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Repartir un total en grupos iguales.
vocabulary.cloze: To find a unit rate, I ___ the price by the number of units.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Chia một lượng thành các phần bằng nhau.

launch.narrative: You're comparing ticket prices at different game booths in the arcade. Booth A charges $3 for 5 games, and Booth B charges $5 for 8 games. Which booth gives you a better deal? To find out, you need to figure out the cost per game — that's the unit rate!
launch.contextImage: Two colorful arcade booths with price signs showing different rates
noticePrompts: What two quantities are being compared at each booth?
Which booth seems like a better deal at first glance?
wonderPrompts: How can you compare prices when the number of games is different?
What does it mean to find the cost for just one game?

conceptIntro.heading: What is a unit rate, and how does it help me find the better buy?
conceptIntro.intro: A rate compares two amounts with different units. A unit rate tells you the amount for just 1 of something, like the cost for 1 game.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: Booth A charges $3 for 5 games. I want the cost for just 1 game.
I divide the cost by the games: $3 ÷ 5 = $0.60.
So Booth A costs $0.60 per game. That is the unit rate.
Now I can compare this booth fairly with any other booth.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: A booth charges $4 for 8 games. What do we divide?
What is the cost for just 1 game? ($0.50 per game.)

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table, find the cost per game for each booth by dividing.
Then decide which booth has the lowest unit rate — that is the better buy.

explore.instructions: Calculate the unit rate (cost per game) for each arcade booth. Divide the total cost by the number of games.

discourse.prompt: How does finding the unit rate help you compare the booths?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The best deal is Booth ___ because its unit rate of ___ per game is the ___.

optional.stem: The Star Blaster cabinet scored 90 points in 3 games. What is the unit rate in points per game?
optional.explanation: Divide points by games: 90 ÷ 3 = 30 points per game.

optional.stem: Two cabinets give points over time. Nova scored 78 points in 6 minutes. Pulse scored 75 points in 5 minutes. Which cabinet has the faster scoring rate (more points per minute)?
choices: They score at the same rate
optional.explanation: Nova: 78 ÷ 6 = 13 points per minute. Pulse: 75 ÷ 5 = 15 points per minute. 15 > 13, so Pulse is faster.

optional.label: Match each arcade deal to its unit rate (coins per play).

optional.stem: Two claw machines cost coins per play. Machine A charges 150 coins for 5 plays. Machine B charges 168 coins for 8 plays. Which machine is the better value (fewer coins per play)?
choices: Machine B, at 21 coins per play
Machine A, at 30 coins per play
Machine A, because it costs fewer coins total
optional.explanation: Machine A: 150 ÷ 5 = 30 coins per play. Machine B: 168 ÷ 8 = 21 coins per play. 21 < 30, so Machine B is the better value.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Inverted Unit Rate at the Ticket Booth

workedExample.label: Find the scoring rate in points per minute: 96 points in 6 minutes
Write the unit rate
workedExample.work: The unit rate is 0.0625 points per minute

optional.correctWork: To find points per minute, divide points by minutes: 96 ÷ 6 = 16 points per minute. The student divided minutes by points (the wrong way), which gives minutes per point, not points per minute.
hints: For 'points per minute,' which quantity should be on top — the points or the minutes?
Divide the first quantity by the second: 96 points ÷ 6 minutes = 16 points per minute.

approaching.stem: A booth charges $4.50 for 9 games. What is the unit rate?
A car travels 180 miles using 6 gallons of gas. What is the unit rate in miles per gallon?
A pack of 8 markers costs $6.00. What is the cost per marker?

approaching.explanation: A unit rate has 1 in the second quantity. '12 miles per 1 hour' is the only choice where the second quantity is 1.

approaching.instructions: Find the unit rate for each arcade deal.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Rates and Unit Rates.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

onLevel.instructions: Complete the table to find unit rates and determine the best deal.

onLevel.stem: Which token pack from the table above is the best deal?
onLevel.explanation: Pack B has a unit rate of $0.20 per token, which is the lowest cost per token.

onLevel.instructions: Sort each situation by whether the unit rate is 'per item' or 'per time'.

onLevel.stem: Runner A completes 4 laps in 12 minutes. Runner B completes 5 laps in 14 minutes. Who runs more laps per minute?
onLevel.explanation: Runner A: 4÷12 ≈ 0.33 laps/min. Runner B: 5÷14 ≈ 0.36 laps/min. Runner B runs slightly more laps per minute.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Rates and Unit Rates.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy.

extending.title: Find the Rate Error

workedExample.label: Find the unit rate: 12 tickets for $8
Write the unit rate
workedExample.work: The unit rate is $1.50 per ticket

extending.correctWork: To find cost per ticket, divide $8 ÷ 12 = $0.67 per ticket (rounded). The student divided tickets by cost instead of cost by tickets.
hints: What should go in the numerator — the cost or the tickets?
To find 'dollars per ticket,' divide dollars by tickets: $8 ÷ 12 ≈ $0.67 per ticket.

extending.prompt: An arcade sells tokens in three packs: 25 tokens for $5, 60 tokens for $10.80, and 100 tokens for $19. A student says the 100-token pack is always the best deal because you get the most tokens. Is the student correct? Find all three unit rates and explain.
extending.sentenceFrame: The student is ___ because the best deal is the ___ pack with a unit rate of $___ per token.

extending.instructions: Two water fountains fill bottles at different rates. Fountain A fills 3 bottles in 5 minutes. Fountain B fills 7 bottles in 10 minutes. Compare their rates.

optionalActivity.name: Arcade Unit Rate Scoreboard
optionalActivity.intro: Climb the arcade scoreboard by finding the unit rate at each game cabinet!

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Rates and Unit Rates is skipping the key idea: "To find a unit rate, divide the total cost by the number of items. The lower cost per item is the better buy." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: The arcade snack bar sells popcorn in two sizes: a small bag with 3 cups for $2.25 and a large bag with 8 cups for $5.60. You want the most popcorn for your money.
connect.promptQuestion: Which size is the better deal, and how do you know?
connect.prompt: The ___ bag is the better deal because its unit rate is $___ per cup, which is ___ than $___ per cup for the other size.

exitTicket.stem: A store sells 6 pencils for $1.50 and 10 pencils for $2.80. Which is the better buy?
choices: They cost the same per pencil
exitTicket.explanation: $1.50 ÷ 6 = $0.25 per pencil. $2.80 ÷ 10 = $0.28 per pencil. $0.25 < $0.28, so 6 for $1.50 is the better buy.

printables.name: Unit Rate Shopping War
printables.desc: Partners flip cards, compute unit rates, and the better price or faster rate wins each round.

printables.name: Rates and Unit Rates — Color by Number
printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.

printables.name: Rates and Unit Rates — Vocabulary Word Search
printables.desc: Find this lesson's key vocabulary terms in the puzzle.

printables.name: Rates and Unit Rates — MCAP-Style Practice
printables.desc: MCAP-style selected- and constructed-response practice items.

graphicNovel.title: Axiom City: The Scaling Stage
graphicNovel.desc: Interactive Axiom City episode — read straight through; gates coach but never block. Not graded.
